NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
text.Rotherham: The oracle, on Nineveh,—the scroll of the vision of Nahum, the Elkoshite.
text.KJV: 1:1 The burden of Nineueh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
text.DRV: 1:1 The burden of Ninive. The Book of the vision of Nahum the Elceseite.

text.NRSV: A jealous and avenging God is the Lord, the Lord is avenging and wrathful; the Lord takes vengeance on his adversaries and keeps wrath for his enemies.
text.Rotherham: A GOD jealous and avenging, is Yahweh, an avenger, is Yahweh, and a lord of wrath, an avenger, is Yahweh towards his adversaries, and, a retainer [of anger], is he to his foes.
text.KJV: 1:2 God is ielous, and the Lord reuengeth: the Lord reuengeth, and is furious, the Lord wil take vengeance on his aduersaries, and he reserueth wrath for his enemies.
text.DRV: 1:2 God is jealous, & our Lord revenging, our Lord revenging, and having fury: our Lord revenging on his adversaries, and he is angry with his enemies.

text.NRSV: The Lord is slow to anger but great in power, and the Lord will by no means clear the guilty. His way is in whirlwind and storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, is slow to anger, but great in vigour, He will not leave, unpunished, as for Yahweh, in storm-wind and in tempest, is his way, and, clouds, are the dust of his feet.
text.KJV: 1:3 The Lord is slow to anger, and great in power, and will not at all acquit the wicked: the Lord hath his way in the whirlewind, and in the storme, and the clouds are the dust of his feete.
text.DRV: 1:3 Our Lord is patient, and great of strength, and cleansing, he will not make innocent. Our Lord's ways in tempest, & whirlwind, & clouds, the dust of his feet.

text.NRSV: He rebukes the sea and makes it dry, and he drains all the rivers; Bashan and Carmel wither, and the bloom of Lebanon fades.
text.Rotherham: Who rebuketh the sea and hath made it dry, and, all the streams, hath he dried up,—withered are Bashan and Carmel, Even the bloom of Lebanon, is withered:
text.KJV: 1:4 He rebuketh the sea, and maketh it drie, and drieth vp all the riuers: Bashan languisheth, and Carmel, and the floure of Lebanon languisheth.
text.DRV: 1:4 Rebuking the sea, & drying it up: & bringing all rivers to a desert. Basan is weakened & Carmel: & the flower of Libanus is faded.

text.NRSV: The mountains quake before him, and the hills melt; the earth heaves before him, the world and all who live in it.
text.Rotherham: Mountains, have trembled because of him, and, the hills, have melted,—and the earth, hath lifted itself up, at his presence, the world also, and all who dwell therein.
text.KJV: 1:5 The mountaines quake at him, and the hilles melt, and the earth is burnt at his presence, yea the world and all that dwell therein.
text.DRV: 1:5 The mountains were moved at him, and the hills were desolate: and the earth hath trembled at his presence, & the world, & all that dwell therein.

text.NRSV: Who can stand before his indignation? Who can endure the heat of his anger? His wrath is poured out like fire, and that rocks are shattered by him.
text.Rotherham: Before his indignation, who shall stand? And who shall abide the glow of his anger? His wrath, hath been poured forth like fire, and, the rocks, have been broken down because of him.
text.KJV: 1:6 Who can stand before his indignation? and who can abide in the fiercenesse of his anger? his furie is powred out like fire, and the rocks are throwen downe by him.
text.DRV: 1:6 Before the face of his indignation who shall stand? & who shall resist in the wrath of his fury? His indignation is poured out as fire: and the rocks are dissolved by him.

text.NRSV: The Lord is good, a stronghold in a day of trouble; he protects those who take refuge in him,
text.Rotherham: Good is Yahweh, as a protection in the day of distress,—and one who acknowledgeth them who seek refuge in him.
text.KJV: 1:7 The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble, & he knoweth them that trust in him.
text.DRV: 1:7 Our Lord is good, & strengthening in the day of tribulation: & knowing them that hope in him.

text.NRSV: even in a rushing flood. He will make a full end of his adversaries, and will pursue his enemies into darkness.
text.Rotherham: But, with an overflow rolling on, a full end, will he make of them who rise up against him,—and, his foes, will he pursue into darkness.
text.KJV: 1:8 But with an ouer-running flood he will make an vtter ende of the place thereof, and darkenesse shall pursue his enemies.
text.DRV: 1:8 And in a flood passing by, he will make a consummation of the place thereof: & darkness shall pursue his enemies.

text.NRSV: Why do you plot against the Lord? He will make an utter end; trouble will not rise up a second time.
text.Rotherham: What can ye devise against Yahweh? A full end, is he making,—Distress, shall not rise up twice:
text.KJV: 1:9 What doe ye imagine against the Lord ? he will make an vtter ende: affliction shall not rise vp the second time.
text.DRV: 1:9 What think ye against our Lord? Consummation he will make: there shall not rise double tribulation.

text.NRSV: Like entangled thorns, like drunkards as they drink, they are consumed like dry straw.
text.Rotherham: Though they were like thorns intertwined, and as drunkards drenched with their drink, yet have they been devoured, like stubble fully dry.
text.KJV: 1:10 For while they be folden together as thornes, and while they are drunken as drunkards, they shall be deuoured as stubble fully drie.
text.DRV: 1:10 Because as thorns clasp one another: so the feast of them that drink together: they shall be consumed as stubble full of dryness.

text.NRSV: From you one has gone out who plots evil against the Lord, one who counsels wickedness.
text.Rotherham: Out of thee, hath one come forth—plotting, against Yahweh, wickedness,—A counselor of the Abandoned One.
text.KJV: 1:11 There is one come out of thee, that imagineth euill against the Lord: a wicked counseller.
text.DRV: 1:11 Out of thee shall come forth one that thinketh malice against our Lord in the mind devising prevarication.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, “Though they are at full strength and many, they will be cut off and pass away. Though I have afflicted you, I will afflict you no more.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, Though they be in full force, and so in great numbers, yet, even so, have they been cut off, and have passed away,—If I humble thee [once], I will not humble thee again.
text.KJV: 1:12 Thus saith the Lord, Though they be quiet, and likewise many, yet thus shall they be cut downe, when he shall passe through: though I haue afflicted thee, I will afflict thee no more.
text.DRV: 1:12 Thus saith our Lord: If they shall be perfect: and so a great many, so also shall they be shorn, and he shall pass by: I have afflicted thee, and I will afflict thee no more.

text.NRSV: “And now I will break off his yoke from you and snap the bonds that bind you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, will I break his yoke from off thee,—and, thy fetters, will I tear off.
text.KJV: 1:13 For now will I breake his yoke from off thee, and will burst thy bonds in sunder.
text.DRV: 1:13 And now I will break his rod from off thy back, & I will burst thy bonds in sunder.

text.NRSV: The Lord has commanded concerning you: “Your name shall be perpetuated no longer; from the house of your gods I will cut off the carved image and the cast image. I will make your grave, for you are worthless.”
text.Rotherham: Then will Yahweh give command concerning thee, None of thy name shall be sown any more,—out of the house of thy gods, will I cut off carved image and molten image, I will appoint thy grave, for thou art of little esteem.
text.KJV: 1:14 And the Lord hath giuen a commandement concerning thee, that no more of thy name be sowen: out of the house of thy gods will I cut off the grauen image, and the molten image, I wil make thy graue, for thou art vile.
text.DRV: 1:14 And Our Lord will command upon thee, there shall not be sown of thy name any more: out of the house of thy God I will destroy sculptile, & molten, I will put thy grave, because thou art dishonoured.

text.NRSV: Look! On the mountains the feet of one who brings good tidings, who proclaims peace! Celebrate your festivals, O Judah, fulfill your vows, for never again shall the wicked invade you; they are utterly cut off.
text.Rotherham: Lo! upon the mountains, The feet of one, who bringeth Good Tidings! who publisheth Prosperity! Celebrate, O Judah, thy pilgrim festivals, fulfil thy vows, for, not again, any more, shall the Abandoned One, pass through thee, he hath been wholly cut off.
text.KJV: 1:15 Behold vpon the mountaines the feete of him that bringeth good tidings, that publisheth peace. O Iudah keepe thy solemne feasts, performe thy vowes: for the wicked shall no more passe through thee, he is vtterly cut off.
text.DRV: 1:15 Behold upon the mountains the feet of him that Evangelizeth, and preacheth peace: celebrate, o Juda, thy festivities, and render thy vows: because Belial shall no more add to pass through thee, he is wholly perished.

text.NRSV: A shatterer has come up against you. Guard the ramparts; watch the road; gird your loins; collect all your strength.
text.Rotherham: He that breaketh in pieces hath come up over thy face, Keep the keeps,—watch the way, brace the loins, make vigour very firm.
text.KJV: 2:1 He that dasheth in pieces is come up before thy face: keep the munition, watch the way: make thy loines strong: fortifie thy power mightily.
text.DRV: 2:1 He is come up that shall scatter before thee, that shall keep the siege: behold the way, fortify the loins, strengthen force exceedingly.

text.NRSV: (For the Lord is restoring the majesty of Jacob, as well as the majesty of Israel, though ravagers have ravaged them and ruined their branches.)
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh hath restored the excellency of Jacob, like the excellency of Israel,—for the plunderers have plundered them, and, their vine branches, have they marred.
text.KJV: 2:2 For the Lord hath turned away the excellencie of Iacob, as the excellencie of Israel: for the emptiers haue emptied them out, and marred their vine branches.
text.DRV: 2:2 Because our Lord hath rendered the pride of Jacob, as the pride of Israel: because the waters have dissipated them, & have marred their branches.

text.NRSV: The shields of his warriors are red; his soldiers are clothed in crimson. The metal on the chariots flashes like fire on the day of his preparation; the chargers prance.
text.Rotherham: The shield of his heroes, is made red, the men of war, are clad in crimson, on fire, are the chariot-steels, on the day he maketh ready,—and, the lances, are put in motion.
text.KJV: 2:3 The shield of his mightie men is made red, the valiant men are in scarlet: the charets shall bee with flaming torches in the day of his preparation, and the firre trees shall bee terribly shaken.
text.DRV: 2:3 The buckler of his valiants fiery, the men of the army in scarlets, the reins of the chariot fiery in the day of his preparation, & the drivers are brought asleep.

text.NRSV: The chariots race madly through the streets, they rush to and fro through the squares; their appearance is like torches, they dart like lightning.
text.Rotherham: In the streets, madly go the chariots, they rush along in the broadways,—their appearance, is like torches, as lightnings, hither and thither do they run.
text.KJV: 2:4 The charets shall rage in the streets, they shall iustle one against another in the broad wayes: they shall seeme like torches, they shall runne like the lightnings.
text.DRV: 2:4 In the ways they are troubled, the chariots struck one against another in the streets: their appearance are as it were lamps, as it were lightning running to & fro.

text.NRSV: The officers are summoned, they stumble as they go forward; they hasten to the wall, and the mantelet is set up.
text.Rotherham: Let him call to mind his nobles, they shall stumble as they go—let them hasten to her wall, yet the storming cover, is prepared.
text.KJV: 2:5 Hee shall recount his worthies: they shall stumble in their walke: they shall make haste to the wal thereof, and the defence shall bee prepared.
text.DRV: 2:5 He will remember his valiants, they shall fall in their ways: they shall quickly scale the walls thereof, and a bower shall be prepared.

text.NRSV: The river gates are opened; the palace quakes.
text.Rotherham: The gates of the rivers, have been opened,—and, the palace, doth quake.
text.KJV: 2:6 The gates of the riuers shall bee opened, and the palace shall bee dissolued.
text.DRV: 2:6 The gates of the rivers are opened, and the temple thrown down to the ground.

text.NRSV: It is decreed that the city be exiled, its women led away, moaning like doves and beating their breasts.
text.Rotherham: And, Huzzab, hath been taken captive—hath been led up,—and, her handmaids, are making a moan like the sound of doves, as they taber upon their heart.
text.KJV: 2:7 And Huzzab shall be led away captiue, she shall be brought vp, and her maids shall leade her as with the voyce of doues, tabring vpon their breasts.
text.DRV: 2:7 And the soldier is led away captive: and her handmaids were led away mourning as doves, murmuring in their hearts.

text.NRSV: Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. “Halt! Halt!”—but no one turns back.
text.Rotherham: Yet, as for Nineveh, like a reservoir of waters, are her waters,—but, those men, are in flight! Stand! stand!! but no one is turning.
text.KJV: 2:8 But Nineueh is of olde like a poole of water: yet they shall flee away. Stand, stand shall they cry: but none shal looke backe.
text.DRV: 2:8 And Ninive her waters as it were a fishpool of waters: but themselves are fled. Stand stand, and there is none that will return.

text.NRSV: “Plunder the silver, plunder the gold! There is no end of treasure! An abundance of every precious thing!”
text.Rotherham: Plunder silver, plunder gold,—and there is no end to the costly furnishing, rich with every article of delight.
text.KJV: 2:9 Take ye the spoyle of siluer, take the spoile of golde: for there is none end of the store, and glory out of all the pleasant furniture.
text.DRV: 2:9 Spoil the silver, spoil the gold: and there is no end of the riches of all vessels that are to be desired.

text.NRSV: Desolation, devastation, and destruction! Hearts faint and knees tremble, all loins quake, all faces grow pale!
text.Rotherham: Emptiness, yea turned to emptiness, aye deserted [is she] ! with, heart, unnerved, and, a tottering, of knees, and, anguish, in all loins, and, the faces of them all, have withdrawn their colour.
text.KJV: 2:10 Shee is emptie, and voide, and waste, and the heart melteth, and the knees smite together, and much paine is in all loynes, and the faces of them all gather blacknesse.
text.DRV: 2:10 She is dissipated, and rent, and torn: & pining heart, and dissolution of the knees, and defect in all reins: and the faces of them all as the blackness of a pot.

text.NRSV: What has become of the lions’ den, the cave of the young lions, where the lion goes, and the lion’s whelp, with no one to disturb them?
text.Rotherham: Where is the lair of the lions? Yea the very feeding-place of the young lions,—where walked the lion, the lioness, the lion’s whelp, with none to make them afraid?
text.KJV: 2:11 Where is the dwelling of the Lions, and the feeding place of the yong Lions? where the Lion, euen the olde Lion walked, and the Lions whelpe, and none made them afraid.
text.DRV: 2:11 Where is the habitation of lions, and the pasture of lions' whelps, to which the lion went, to go in thither, the lion's whelp, and is there none to make them afraid?

text.NRSV: The lion has torn enough for his whelps and strangled prey for his lionesses; he has filled his caves with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
text.Rotherham: The lion, used to tear in pieces enough for his whelps, and to strangle for his lionesses,—and then fill with prey his holes, and his lairs with what he had torn.
text.KJV: 2:12 The Lion did teare in pieces enough for his whelpes, and strangled for his Lionesses, and filled his holes with pray, and his dens with rauine.
text.DRV: 2:12 The lion hath caught sufficiently for his whelps, and hath killed for his lionesses: and he hath filled his dens with prey, and his couch with ravening.

text.NRSV: See, I am against you, says the Lord of hosts, and I will burn your chariots in smoke, and the sword shall devour your young lions; I will cut off your prey from the earth, and the voice of your messengers shall be heard no more.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee, Declareth Yahweh of hosts, Therefore will I burn up in smoke her chariots, and, thy young lions, shall be devoured by the sword,—so will I cut off, out of the earth, thy prey, nor shall be heard any more, the voice of thine envoy.
text.KJV: 2:13 Behold, I am against thee, saith the Lord of hosts, and I will burne her charets in the smoke, and the sword shall deuoure thy yong Lions, and I wil cut off thy pray from the earth, and the voice of thy messengers shall no more be heard.
text.DRV: 2:13 Behold I to thee, saith the Lord of hosts, and I will burn thy chariots even to smoke, and the sword shall eat thy little lions: and I will destroy thy prey out of the land, and the voice of thy messengers shall be heard no more.

text.NRSV: Ah! City of bloodshed, utterly deceitful, full of booty— no end to the plunder!
text.Rotherham: Alas for the city of bloodshed! All of it, deceit, of violence full, none releaseth prey!
text.KJV: 3:1 Woe to the bloody City, it is all full of lyes and robberie, the pray departeth not.
text.DRV: 3:1 Woe to thee o city of bloods, all of lying full of tearing: spoil shall not depart from thee.

text.NRSV: The crack of whip and rumble of wheel, galloping horse and bounding chariot!
text.Rotherham: The sound of the whip, and the sound of the rushing wheel,—and horse galloping, and dancing chariot rattling along.
text.KJV: 3:2 The noise of a whip, and the noise of the rattling of the wheeles, and of the praunsing horses, and of the iumping charets.
text.DRV: 3:2 The voice of the whip, and the voice of the violence of the wheel, and of the neighing horse, and of the fervent chariot, and of the horseman mounting.

text.NRSV: Horsemen charging, flashing sword and glittering spear, piles of slain, heaps of corpses, dead bodies without end— they stumble over the bodies!
text.Rotherham: Horsemen uplifting both the flashing sword, and the lightning spear, Aye, a mass of slain, and a weight of dead bridles,—and no end of corpses, so that they stumble upon their corpses.
text.KJV: 3:3 The horseman lifteth vp both the bright sword, & the glittering speare, and there is a multitude of slaine, and a great number of carkeises: and there is none ende of their corpses: they stumble vpon their corpses,
text.DRV: 3:3 And of the glistering sword, and of the glittering spear, and of a multitude slain, and of a grievous ruin: neither is there an end of corpses, and they shall fall on their bodies.

text.NRSV: Because of the countless debaucheries of the prostitute, gracefully alluring, mistress of sorcery, who enslaves nations through her debaucheries, and peoples through her sorcery,
text.Rotherham: Because of the multitude of the unchaste doings of the unchaste one, fair in grace, mistress of secret arts,—who hath been selling nations by her unchaste doings, families by her secret arts,
text.KJV: 3:4 Because of the multitude of the whoredomes of the wel-fauoured harlot, the mistresse of witchcrafts, that selleth nations through her whoredomes, and families through her witchcrafts.
text.DRV: 3:4 Because of the multitude of the fornications, of the harlot beautiful and grateful, and having sorceries, which hath sold nations in her fornications, and families in her sorceries.

text.NRSV: I am against you, says the Lord of hosts, and will lift up your skirts over your face; and I will let nations look on your nakedness and kingdoms on your shame.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee, Declareth Yahweh of hosts, Therefore will I remove thy shirts over thy face,—and let, nations, see thy nakedness, and, kingdoms, thy shame;
text.KJV: 3:5 Behold, I am against thee, saith the Lord of hostes, and I will discouer thy skirtes vpon thy face, and I will shew the nations thy nakednesse, and the kingdomes thy shame.
text.DRV: 3:5 Behold I to thee, saith the Lord of hosts: and I will discover thy privy parts in thy face, and will shew to the Gentiles thy nakedness, and to kingdoms thine ignominy.

text.NRSV: I will throw filth at you and treat you with contempt, and make you a spectacle.
text.Rotherham: And I will cast upon thee abominable filth, and treat thee as foolish,—and set thee as a gazing-stock.
text.KJV: 3:6 And I will cast abominable filth vpon thee, and make thee vile, and will set thee as a gazing stocke.
text.DRV: 3:6 And I will throw upon thee abominations, and will use thee contumeliously, and will put thee for an example.

text.NRSV: Then all who see you will shrink from you and say, “Nineveh is devastated; who will bemoan her?” Where shall I seek comforters for you?
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass that, all who see thee, shall flee from thee, and shall say, Destroyed is Nineveh! Who will bemoan her?—Whence shall I seek any to comfort thee?
text.KJV: 3:7 And it shall come to passe, that all they that looke vpon thee, shall flee from thee, and say; Nineueh is layde waste, who will bemoane her? whence shall I seeke comforters for thee?
text.DRV: 3:7 And it shall be, every one that shall see thee, shall leap back from thee, and shall say: Ninive is wasted: who shall shake the head upon thee? Whence shall I seek a comforter for thee?

text.NRSV: Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile, with water around her, her rampart a sea, water her wall?
text.Rotherham: Art thou better than No-amon, who sat among the Nile-streams, waters round about her,—whose fortress was the sea, from the sea, her wall.
text.KJV: 3:8 Art thou better then populous No, that was scituate among the riuers that had the waters round about it, whose rampart was the sea, and her wall was from the sea?
text.DRV: 3:8 Why, art thou better than Alexandria full of peoples, which dwelleth in the rivers? Waters round about it: whose riches, the sea: waters the walls thereof.

text.NRSV: Ethiopia was her strength, Egypt too, and that without limit; Put and the Libyans were her helpers.
text.Rotherham: Ethiopia, was her strength, and Egypt—Yea, without end,—Put and Lubim, were among thy helpers.
text.KJV: 3:9 Ethiopia and Egypt were her strength, and it was infinit, Put and Lubim were thy helpers.
text.DRV: 3:9 Æthiopia the strength of it, and Ægypt, and there is no end: Afrike, and the Libyans have been in thine aid.

text.NRSV: Yet she went into exile, she went into captivity; her infants were dashed in pieces at the head of every street; lots were cast for her nobles, and all her dignitaries were bound in fetters.
text.Rotherham: Yet, she, was given up to exile, she went into captivity, even her babes, were dashed to the ground, at the head of all the streets,—and, for her honourable men, cast they lots, and, all her great men, were bound together in chains.
text.KJV: 3:10 Yet was she caried away, she went into captiuitie: her yong children also were dashed in pieces at the top of all the streetes: and they cast lots for her honourable men, and all her great men were bound in chaines.
text.DRV: 3:10 But she also in transmigration was led into captivity: her little ones were dashed in the head of all ways, and upon her noble ones, they have cast lot, and all her great men were made fast in fetters.

text.NRSV: You also will be drunken, you will go into hiding; you will seek a refuge from the enemy.
text.Rotherham: Thou too, shalt be drunken, thou shalt hide thyself,—thou too, shalt seek shelter from the foe:
text.KJV: 3:11 Thou also shalt be drunken: thou shalt bee hid, thou also shalt seeke strength because of the enemie.
text.DRV: 3:11 Thou also therefore shalt be made drunk, and shalt be despised: and thou shalt seek help of the enemy.

text.NRSV: All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs— if shaken they fall into the mouth of the eater.
text.Rotherham: All thy fortresses, shall be fig-trees with first-ripe figs: if thy be shaken, then shall [the fruit] fall on the mouth of the eater.
text.KJV: 3:12 All thy strong holds shall be like fig trees with the first ripe figs: if they bee shaken, they shall euen fall into the mouth of the eater.
text.DRV: 3:12 All thy munitions as figtrees with their green figs: if they be shaken, they will fall into the mouth of the eater.

text.NRSV: Look at your troops: they are women in your midst. The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire has consumed your bars.
text.Rotherham: Lo! thy people, are women, in thy midst, to thy foes, have been set wide open the gates of thy and,—a fire, hath devoured, thy bars.
text.KJV: 3:13 Beholde, thy people in the midst of thee are women: the gates of thy land shall be set wide open vnto thine enemies, the fire shall deuoure thy barres.
text.DRV: 3:13 Behold thy people women in the midst of thee: to thine enemies the gates of thy land with opening shall be opened, fire shall devour thy bars.

text.NRSV: Draw water for the siege, strengthen your forts; get into the clay, tread the mortar, take hold of the brick mold!
text.Rotherham: Siege-water, draw for thyself, strengthen thy fortresses,—go into the clay, and tread thou the mortar, make strong the brick.
text.KJV: 3:14 Draw thee waters for the siege: fortifie thy strong holdes, goe into clay, and tread the morter: make strong the bricke-kill.
text.DRV: 3:14 Draw thee water for the siege, build thy munitions: enter into the clay, and tread, making mortar hold the brick.

text.NRSV: There the fire will devour you, the sword will cut you off. It will devour you like the locust. Multiply yourselves like the locust, multiply like the grasshopper!
text.Rotherham: There, shall a fire devour thee, the sword, shall cut thee off, it shall devour thee like the grass locust, make thyself numerous like the grass locust, make thyself numerous as the swarming locust:
text.KJV: 3:15 There shall the fire deuoure thee: the sword shall cut thee off: it shall eate thee vp like the cankerworme: make thy selfe many as the cankerworme, make thy selfe many as the locusts.
text.DRV: 3:15 There the fire shall eat thee: thou shalt perish by the sword, as a bruke it shall devour thee: be gathered together as the bruke, be multiplied as the locust.

text.NRSV: You increased your merchants more than the stars of the heavens. The locust sheds its skin and flies away.
text.Rotherham: Though thou have multiplied thy foot-soldiers beyond the stars of the heavens, the grass locust, hath stript itself and flown away!
text.KJV: 3:16 Thou hast multiplied thy merchants aboue the starres of heauen; the cankerworme spoileth & flieth away.
text.DRV: 3:16 Thou hast made thy merchandises more than are the stars of heaven: the bruke was spread, and flew away.

text.NRSV: Your guards are like grasshoppers, your scribes like swarms of locusts settling on the fences on a cold day— when the sun rises, they fly away; no one knows where they have gone.
text.Rotherham: Thy mercenary crowds, are like the swarming locust, and, thy mixed multitudes, like locusts—swarms of locusts,—which settle in the hedges on a cold day, the sun, hath broken forth, and they are in flight, and unknown is the place where they are!
text.KJV: 3:17 The crowned are as the locusts, and thy captains as the great grashoppers which campe in the hedges in the cold day: but when the Sunne ariseth, they flee away, and their place is not knowen where they are.
text.DRV: 3:17 Thy keepers as the locusts: and thy little ones as it were the locusts of locusts, which swarm on the hedges in the day of cold: the sun arose, and they flew away, and their place was not known where they were.

text.NRSV: Your shepherds are asleep, O king of Assyria; your nobles slumber. Your people are scattered on the mountains with no one to gather them.
text.Rotherham: Asleep are thy shepherds, O king of Assyria, thy nobles, must needs rest. Scattered are thy people upon the mountains, and there is none to gather them.
text.KJV: 3:18 Thy shepheards slumber, O king of Assyria: thy nobles shall dwell in the dust: thy people is scattered vpon the mountaines, & no man gathereth them.
text.DRV: 3:18 Thy pastors slumbered, o king of Assur, thy princes shall be buried, thy people lay hid in the mountains, and there is none to gather them together.

text.NRSV: There is no assuaging your hurt, your wound is mortal. All who hear the news about you clap their hands over you. For who has ever escaped your endless cruelty?
text.Rotherham: No lessening of thine injury, grievous is thy wound,—all who have heard the report of thee, have clapped their hands over thee, for, upon whom, hath not thy cruelty passed without ceasing?
text.KJV: 3:19 There is no healing of thy bruise: thy wound is grieuous: all that heare the bruit of thee, shall clap the hands ouer thee; for vpon whom hath not thy wickednesse passed continually?
text.DRV: 3:19 Thy destruction is not obscure, thy plague is very sore: all that have heard the fame of thee, have clapped the hand upon thee: for upon whom hath not thy malice passed always?
